title: At a glance: Departmental Evaluation Plan for the Public Health Agency of Canada 2025-2026 to 2029-2030 - Canada.ca
content:
## At a glance: Departmental Evaluation Plan for the Public Health Agency of Canada 2025-2026 to 2029-2030
The Office of Audit and Evaluation (OAE) Departmental Evaluation Plan (DEP) for the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) describes the Program Evaluation Division's scheduled work for the five-year period from 2025-2026 to 2029-2030. The Plan was developed to meet PHAC's commitments under the Policy on Results (2016) which requires that each department prepare a five-year plan that is updated annually.
The projects included in this plan ensure full coverage of all grants and contributions spending over $5 million annually over a five-year period, commitments included in Treasury Board Submissions, information needs of program management, program risks, and the optimal use of resources. The Departmental Results Framework (DRF), program inventory, and Performance Information Profiles developed by PHAC have informed the development of this plan.

## Planning for evaluation
Over the next five years, OAE plans to conduct 30 evaluations involving PHAC; of these, 21 will focus on PHAC activities and two will examine the activities of both PHAC and Health Canada. The remaining seven are horizontal evaluations, five of which will be led by other government departments (OGDs), and two by Health Canada. A yearly breakdown is provided in Table 1. Table 2 provides a breakdown of the coverage of direct program spending by core responsibility for all scheduled evaluations over the next five years.

## Table 1: Number of evaluations per fiscal year 2025-2026 to 2029-2030

Note: Detailed estimates on program budgets are based on annual data for 2025-2026. This financial data was extracted by the Chief Financial Officer and Corporate Management Branch in early May, 2025. As a result, these budget figures may not fully align with those contained in other corporate reporting documents such as the Departmental Plan, the Departmental Results Report, or the Three-Year Transfer Payment Program Plan.
